english_name: The Second Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Corinthians

verses.latin_vulgate: Utinam sustineretis modicum quid insipientiæ meæ, sed et supportare me:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὄφελον ἀνείχεσθέ μου μικρόν τῇ ἀφροσύνη ἀλλὰ καὶ ἀνέχεσθέ μου
verses.king_james: Would to God ye could bear with me a little in *my* folly: and indeed bear with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: æmulor enim vos Dei æmulatione. Despondi enim vos uni viro, virginem castam exhibere Christo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ζηλῶ γὰρ ὑμᾶς θεοῦ ζήλῳ ἡρμοσάμην γὰρ ὑμᾶς ἑνὶ ἀνδρὶ παρθένον ἁγνὴν παραστῆσαι τῷ χριστῷ
verses.king_james: For I am jealous over you with godly jealousy: for I have espoused you to one husband, that I may present *you as* a chaste virgin to Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Timeo autem ne sicut serpens Hevam seduxit astutia sua, ita corrumpantur sensus vestri, et excidant a simplicitate, quæ est in Christo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: φοβοῦμαι δὲ μήπως ὡς ὁ ὄφις εὕαν ἐξηπάτησεν ἐν τῇ πανουργίᾳ αὐτοῦ οὕτως φθαρῇ τὰ νοήματα ὑμῶν ἀπὸ τῆς ἁπλότητος τῆς εἰς τὸν χριστόν
verses.king_james: But I fear, lest by any means, as the serpent beguiled Eve through his subtilty, so your minds should be corrupted from the simplicity that is in Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam si is qui venit, alium Christum prædicat, quem non prædicavimus, aut alium spiritum accipitis, quem non accepistis: aut aliud Evangelium, quod non recepistis: recte pateremini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ μὲν γὰρ ὁ ἐρχόμενος ἄλλον ἰησοῦν κηρύσσει ὃν οὐκ ἐκηρύξαμεν ἢ πνεῦμα ἕτερον λαμβάνετε ὃ οὐκ ἐλάβετε ἢ εὐαγγέλιον ἕτερον ὃ οὐκ ἐδέξασθε καλῶς ἠνείχεσθε
verses.king_james: For if he that cometh preacheth another Jesus, whom we have not preached, or *if* ye receive another spirit, which ye have not received, or another gospel, which ye have not accepted, ye might well bear with *him*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Existimo enim nihil me minus fecisse a magnis Apostolis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λογίζομαι γὰρ μηδὲν ὑστερηκέναι τῶν ὑπερ λίαν ἀποστόλων
verses.king_james: For I suppose I was not a whit behind the very chiefest apostles.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam etsi imperitus sermone, sed non scientia, in omnibus autem manifestati sumus vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ καὶ ἰδιώτης τῷ λόγῳ ἀλλ᾽ οὐ τῇ γνώσει ἀλλ᾽ ἐν παντὶ φανερωθέντες ἐν πᾶσιν εἰς ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: But though *I be* rude in speech, yet not in knowledge; but we have been throughly made manifest among you in all things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aut numquid peccatum feci, meipsum humilians, ut vos exaltemini? quoniam gratis Evangelium Dei evangelizavi vobis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ ἁμαρτίαν ἐποίησα ἐμαυτὸν ταπεινῶν ἵνα ὑμεῖς ὑψωθῆτε ὅτι δωρεὰν τὸ τοῦ θεοῦ εὐαγγέλιον εὐηγγελισάμην ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: Have I committed an offence in abasing myself that ye might be exalted, because I have preached to you the gospel of God freely?

verses.latin_vulgate: Alias ecclesias expoliavi, accipiens stipendium ad ministerium vestrum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄλλας ἐκκλησίας ἐσύλησα λαβὼν ὀψώνιον πρὸς τὴν ὑμῶν διακονίαν
verses.king_james: I robbed other churches, taking wages *of them*, to do you service.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum essem apud vos, et egerem, nulli onerosus fui: nam quod mihi deerat, suppleverunt fratres, qui venerunt a Macedonia: et in omnibus sine onere me vobis servavi, et servabo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ παρὼν πρὸς ὑμᾶς καὶ ὑστερηθεὶς οὐ κατενάρκησα οὐδενός τὸ γὰρ ὑστέρημά μου προσανεπλήρωσαν οἱ ἀδελφοὶ ἐλθόντες ἀπὸ μακεδονίας καὶ ἐν παντὶ ἀβαρῆ ὑμῖν ἐμαυτὸν ἐτήρησα καὶ τηρήσω
verses.king_james: And when I was present with you, and wanted, I was chargeable to no man: for that which was lacking to me the brethren which came from Macedonia supplied: and in all *things* I have kept myself from being burdensome unto you, and *so* will I keep *myself*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Est veritas Christi in me, quoniam hæc gloriatio non infringetur in me in regionibus Achaiæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔστιν ἀλήθεια χριστοῦ ἐν ἐμοὶ ὅτι ἡ καύχησις αὕτη οὐ σφραγίσεται εἰς ἐμὲ ἐν τοῖς κλίμασιν τῆς ἀχαΐας
verses.king_james: As the truth of Christ is in me, no man shall stop me of this boasting in the regions of Achaia.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare? quia non diligo vos? Deus scit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διατί ὅτι οὐκ ἀγαπῶ ὑμᾶς ὁ θεὸς οἶδεν
verses.king_james: Wherefore? because I love you not? God knoweth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod autem facio, et faciam: ut amputem occasionem eorum qui volunt occasionem, ut in quo gloriantur, inveniantur sicut et nos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅ δὲ ποιῶ καὶ ποιήσω ἵνα ἐκκόψω τὴν ἀφορμὴν τῶν θελόντων ἀφορμήν ἵνα ἐν ᾧ καυχῶνται εὑρεθῶσιν καθὼς καὶ ἡμεῖς
verses.king_james: But what I do, that I will do, that I may cut off occasion from them which desire occasion; that wherein they glory, they may be found even as we.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam ejusmodi pseudoapostoli sunt operarii subdoli, transfigurantes se in apostolos Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ γὰρ τοιοῦτοι ψευδαπόστολοι ἐργάται δόλιοι μετασχηματιζόμενοι εἰς ἀποστόλους χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: For such *are* false apostles, deceitful workers, transforming themselves into the apostles of Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non mirum: ipse enim Satanas transfigurat se in angelum lucis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐ θαυμαστόν αὐτὸς γὰρ ὁ σατανᾶς μετασχηματίζεται εἰς ἄγγελον φωτός
verses.king_james: And no marvel; for Satan himself is transformed into an angel of light.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est ergo magnum, si ministri ejus transfigurentur velut ministri justitiæ: quorum finis erit secundum opera ipsorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ μέγα οὖν εἰ καὶ οἱ διάκονοι αὐτοῦ μετασχηματίζονται ὡς διάκονοι δικαιοσύνης ὧν τὸ τέλος ἔσται κατὰ τὰ ἔργα αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: Therefore *it is* no great thing if his ministers also be transformed as the ministers of righteousness; whose end shall be according to their works.

verses.latin_vulgate: Iterum dico (ne quis me putet insipientem esse, alioquin velut insipientem accipite me, ut et ego modicum quid glorier),
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάλιν λέγω μή τίς με δόξῃ ἄφρονα εἶναι εἰ δὲ μήγε κἂν ὡς ἄφρονα δέξασθέ με ἵνα μικρόν τι κἀγὼ καυχήσωμαι
verses.king_james: I say again, Let no man think me a fool; if otherwise, yet as a fool receive me, that I may boast myself a little.

verses.latin_vulgate: quod loquor, non loquor secundum Deum, sed quasi in insipientia, in hac substantia gloriæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃ λαλῶ οὐ λαλῶ κατὰ κύριον ἀλλ᾽ ὡς ἐν ἀφροσύνῃ ἐν ταύτῃ τῇ ὑποστάσει τῆς καυχήσεως
verses.king_james: That which I speak, I speak *it* not after the Lord, but as it were foolishly, in this confidence of boasting.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quoniam multi gloriantur secundum carnem: et ego gloriabor.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπεὶ πολλοὶ καυχῶνται κατὰ τήν σάρκα κἀγὼ καυχήσομαι
verses.king_james: Seeing that many glory after the flesh, I will glory also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Libenter enim suffertis insipientes, cum sitis ipsi sapientes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡδέως γὰρ ἀνέχεσθε τῶν ἀφρόνων φρόνιμοι ὄντες
verses.king_james: For ye suffer fools gladly, seeing ye *yourselves* are wise.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sustinetis enim si quis vos in servitutem redigit, si quis devorat, si quis accipit, si quis extollitur, si quis in faciem vos cædit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνέχεσθε γὰρ εἴ τις ὑμᾶς καταδουλοῖ εἴ τις κατεσθίει εἴ τις λαμβάνει εἴ τις ἐπαίρεται εἴ τις ὑμᾶς εἰς πρόσωπον δέρει
verses.king_james: For ye suffer, if a man bring you into bondage, if a man devour *you*, if a man take *of you*, if a man exalt himself, if a man smite you on the face.

verses.latin_vulgate: Secundum ignobilitatem dico, quasi nos infirmi fuerimus in hac parte. In quo quis audet (in insipientia dico) audeo et ego:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κατὰ ἀτιμίαν λέγω ὡς ὅτι ἡμεῖς ἠσθενήσαμεν ἐν ᾧ δ᾽ ἄν τις τολμᾷ ἐν ἀφροσύνῃ λέγω τολμῶ κἀγώ
verses.king_james: I speak as concerning reproach, as though we had been weak. Howbeit whereinsoever any is bold, (I speak foolishly,) I am bold also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hebræi sunt, et ego: Israëlitæ sunt, et ego: semen Abrahæ sunt, et ego.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἑβραῖοί εἰσιν κἀγώ ἰσραηλῖταί εἰσιν κἀγώ σπέρμα ἀβραάμ εἰσιν κἀγώ
verses.king_james: Are they Hebrews? so *am* I. Are they Israelites? so *am* I. Are they the seed of Abraham? so *am* I.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ministri Christi sunt (ut minus sapiens dico), plus ego: in laboribus plurimis, in carceribus abundantius, in plagis supra modum, in mortibus frequenter.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διάκονοι χριστοῦ εἰσιν παραφρονῶν λαλῶ ὑπὲρ ἐγώ ἐν κόποις περισσοτέρως ἐν πληγαῖς ὑπερβαλλόντως ἐν φυλακαῖς περισσοτέρως ἐν θανάτοις πολλάκις
verses.king_james: Are they ministers of Christ? (I speak as a fool) I *am* more; in labours more abundant, in stripes above measure, in prisons more frequent, in deaths oft.

verses.latin_vulgate: A Jud��is quinquies, quadragenas, una minus, accepi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑπὸ ἰουδαίων πεντάκις τεσσαράκοντα παρὰ μίαν ἔλαβον
verses.king_james: Of the Jews five times received I forty *stripes* save one.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ter virgis cæsus sum, semel lapidatus sum: ter naufragium feci, nocte et die in profundo maris fui,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τρὶς ἐῤῥαβδίσθην, ἅπαξ ἐλιθάσθην τρὶς ἐναυάγησα νυχθήμερον ἐν τῷ βυθῷ πεποίηκα
verses.king_james: Thrice was I beaten with rods, once was I stoned, thrice I suffered shipwreck, a night and a day I have been in the deep;

verses.latin_vulgate: in itineribus sæpe, periculis fluminum, periculis latronum, periculis ex genere, periculis ex gentibus, periculis in civitate, periculis in solitudine, periculis in mari, periculis in falsis fratribus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁδοιπορίαις πολλάκις κινδύνοις ποταμῶν κινδύνοις λῃστῶν κινδύνοις ἐκ γένους κινδύνοις ἐξ ἐθνῶν κινδύνοις ἐν πόλει κινδύνοις ἐν ἐρημίᾳ κινδύνοις ἐν θαλάσσῃ κινδύνοις ἐν ψευδαδέλφοις
verses.king_james: *In* journeyings often, *in* perils of waters, *in* perils of robbers, *in* perils by *mine own* countrymen, *in* perils by the heathen, *in* perils in the city, *in* perils in the wilderness, *in* perils in the sea, *in* perils among false brethren;

verses.latin_vulgate: in labore et ærumna, in vigiliis multis, in fame et siti, in jejuniis multis, in frigore et nuditate,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν κόπῳ καὶ μόχθῳ ἐν ἀγρυπνίαις πολλάκις ἐν λιμῷ καὶ δίψει ἐν νηστείαις πολλάκις ἐν ψύχει καὶ γυμνότητι
verses.king_james: In weariness and painfulness, in watchings often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, in cold and nakedness.

verses.latin_vulgate: præter illa quæ extrinsecus sunt, instantia mea quotidiana, sollicitudo omnium ecclesiarum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: χωρὶς τῶν παρεκτὸς ἡ ἐπισύστασις μου ἡ καθ᾽ ἡμέραν ἡ μέριμνα πασῶν τῶν ἐκκλησιῶν
verses.king_james: Beside those things that are without, that which cometh upon me daily, the care of all the churches.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis infirmatur, et ego non infirmor? quis scandalizatur, et ego non uror?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίς ἀσθενεῖ καὶ οὐκ ἀσθενῶ τίς σκανδαλίζεται καὶ οὐκ ἐγὼ πυροῦμαι
verses.king_james: Who is weak, and I am not weak? who is offended, and I burn not?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si gloriari oportet, quæ infirmitatis meæ sunt, gloriabor.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ καυχᾶσθαι δεῖ τὰ τῆς ἀσθενείας μου καυχήσομαι
verses.king_james: If I must needs glory, I will glory of the things which concern mine infirmities.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus et Pater Domini nostri Jesu Christi, qui est benedictus in sæcula, scit quod non mentior.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ θεὸς καὶ πατὴρ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ οἶδεν ὁ ὢν εὐλογητὸς εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας ὅτι οὐ ψεύδομαι
verses.king_james: The God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, which is blessed for evermore, knoweth that I lie not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Damasci præpositus gentis Aretæ regis custodiebat civitatem Damascenorum ut me comprehenderet:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν δαμασκῷ ὁ ἐθνάρχης ἁρέτα τοῦ βασιλέως ἐφρούρει τὴν δαμασκηνῶν πόλιν πιάσαι με θέλων
verses.king_james: In Damascus the governor under Aretas the king kept the city of the Damascenes with a garrison, desirous to apprehend me:

verses.latin_vulgate: et per fenestram in sporta dimissus sum per murum, et sic effugi manus ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ διὰ θυρίδος ἐν σαργάνῃ ἐχαλάσθην διὰ τοῦ τείχους καὶ ἐξέφυγον τὰς χεῖρας αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And through a window in a basket was I let down by the wall, and escaped his hands.
